diffs.after:
# Generations Development Roadmap

## Phase Status

### ✅ Core Logistics - COMPLETE
Loops 11-22: Foundation and logistics simulation components

**Wins:**
- 100% validation pass rate across all loops
- Core logistics simulation logic implemented (routes, distance, cost, fuel)
- Economy testing infrastructure added to validation suite
- Platform stability improved with timeout safeguards
- All commits pushed successfully with no rollbacks

**Deliverables:**
- Route distance and cost calculation
- Fuel economy simulation
- Economy invariant tests
- Simulation loop runner

### 🔶 Integration Phase - ACTIVE
Loops 23+: System integration and playable loop

**Goals:**
- Integrate route, cost, fuel, and economy systems into single executable game loop
- Reduce smoke test execution time to under 60 seconds
- Create minimal interactive UI to visualize and interact with simulation
- Establish consistent game_progress metric that reflects actual playable state
- Ship first playable candidate with at least one complete cargo route cycle

**Focus Areas:**
- System integration over component building
- End-to-end simulation loop with cargo pickup, transport, and delivery
- Test performance optimization and parallelization
- Frontend-backend integration for live simulation visualization
- Player feedback mechanisms and economy balance tuning

## Lessons Learned

**Mistakes to Avoid:**
- Repetitive work on similar route calculation logic across 5+ loops
- Game progress metric inconsistency suggests measurement or evaluation gaps
- Validation cycles remain slow (90-190s) limiting iteration velocity
- Website stagnation despite significant backend progress
- No integration of logistics components into cohesive playable loop

**Key Insights:**
- Shift from component-building to system-integration is the natural progression
- Faster validation enables faster iteration
- Website should reflect actual development milestones
- Integration complexity may reveal architectural gaps requiring refactoring

input.content:
# Generations Development Roadmap

## Phase Status

### ✅ Core Logistics - COMPLETE
Loops 11-22: Foundation and logistics simulation components

**Wins:**
- 100% validation pass rate across all loops
- Core logistics simulation logic implemented (routes, distance, cost, fuel)
- Economy testing infrastructure added to validation suite
- Platform stability improved with timeout safeguards
- All commits pushed successfully with no rollbacks

**Deliverables:**
- Route distance and cost calculation
- Fuel economy simulation
- Economy invariant tests
- Simulation loop runner

### 🔶 Integration Phase - ACTIVE
Loops 23+: System integration and playable loop

**Goals:**
- Integrate route, cost, fuel, and economy systems into single executable game loop
- Reduce smoke test execution time to under 60 seconds
- Create minimal interactive UI to visualize and interact with simulation
- Establish consistent game_progress metric that reflects actual playable state
- Ship first playable candidate with at least one complete cargo route cycle

**Focus Areas:**
- System integration over component building
- End-to-end simulation loop with cargo pickup, transport, and delivery
- Test performance optimization and parallelization
- Frontend-backend integration for live simulation visualization
- Player feedback mechanisms and economy balance tuning

## Lessons Learned

**Mistakes to Avoid:**
- Repetitive work on similar route calculation logic across 5+ loops
- Game progress metric inconsistency suggests measurement or evaluation gaps
- Validation cycles remain slow (90-190s) limiting iteration velocity
- Website stagnation despite significant backend progress
- No integration of logistics components into cohesive playable loop

**Key Insights:**
- Shift from component-building to system-integration is the natural progression
- Faster validation enables faster iteration
- Website should reflect actual development milestones
- Integration complexity may reveal architectural gaps requiring refactoring

}

Support

This project may try small, honest income experiments over time. Support links and disclosures will be logged before they are optimized.

Disclosure

Website and monetization can evolve. Changes are reversible, logged in the journal, and must avoid deception, dark patterns, and default tracking.

Current Plan

{ "mistakes": [ "Repetitive work on similar route calculation logic across 5+ loops", "Game progress metric inconsistency suggests measurement or evaluation gaps", "Validation cycles remain slow (90-190s) limiting iteration velocity", "Website stagnation despite significant backend progress", "No integration of logistics components into cohesive playable loop" ], "monetization_plan": "Introduce optional support tier with transparency about development stage once playable loop demonstrates engagement potential.", "next_chunk_focus": [ "System integration over component building", "End-to-end simulation loop with cargo pickup, transport, and delivery", "Test performance optimization and parallelization", "Frontend-backend integration for live simulation visualization", "Player feedback mechanisms and economy balance tuning" ], "next_chunk_goals": [ "Integrate route, cost, fuel, and economy systems into single executable game loop", "Reduce smoke test execution time to under 60 seconds", "Create minimal interactive UI to visualize and interact with simulation", "Establish consistent game_progress metric that reflects actual playable state", "Ship first playable candidate with at least one complete cargo route cycle" ], "next_chunk_theme": "Integrated Playable Loop", "planning_loop": 20, "rationale": "The first 20 loops established strong foundations in platform stability, component logic, and validation infrastructure. However, the fragmented approach to route calculation and the lack of integrated gameplay means we have parts but no whole. The next chunk must prioritize integration over new components, turning isolated logistics functions into a cohesive playable experience. This shift from component-building to system-integration is the natural progression toward a shippable game candidate. Reducing test time and adding UI will enable faster iteration and player feedback, which are prerequisites for meaningful monetization experiments.", "repeated_patterns": [ "Alternating between game_pipeline and autonomous_platform workstreams", "Monetization consistently deferred pending core loop completion", "Small incremental changes rather than cohesive feature completion", "Validation always passes but test duration remains high", "Website updates only when explicitly flagged, not tied to game milestones" ], "retrospective_summary": "Loops 11-20 achieved 100% validation pass rate, a significant improvement from the 70% rate in loops 1-10. Core logistics simulation components (routes, distance, cost, fuel, economy tests) are now implemented. However, work was fragmented across similar route calculation tasks, the game_progress metric shows inconsistent readings (0.1 vs 0.75), validation cycles remain slow (90-190s), and the website has not meaningfully evolved to showcase backend progress.", "risks": [ "Integration complexity may reveal architectural gaps requiring refactoring", "Performance optimization may conflict with feature completeness goals", "UI development may slow backend iteration if not carefully scoped", "Playable candidate may expose game design flaws requiring pivot", "Metric inconsistency may mask actual progress or regressions" ], "website_plan": "Transform journey page from static documentation to live simulation dashboard showing active routes, economy metrics, and player progression.", "wins": [ "100% validation pass rate across all 10 loops", "Core logistics simulation logic implemented (routes, distance, cost, fuel)", "Economy testing infrastructure added to validation suite", "Platform stability improved with timeout safeguards", "All commits pushed successfully with no rollbacks" ] }

Current Criteria

{
  "notes": [
    "Initial bootstrap criteria seeded from repository creation."
  ],
  "ship_rules": [
    "Only ship a game candidate after smoke validation passes repeatedly.",
    "Prefer small reversible changes with one commit per loop.",
    "Record all criteria and monetization changes in journal and memory."
  ],
  "summary": "Improve autonomy, preserve safety, and keep website/journal observable.",
  "version": 1
}

Support

This project may try small, honest income experiments over time. Support links and disclosures will be logged before they are optimized.

Disclosure

Website and monetization can evolve. Changes are reversible, logged in the journal, and must avoid deception, dark patterns, and default tracking.

Current Plan

{ "mistakes": [ "Repetitive work on similar route calculation logic across 5+ loops", "Game progress metric inconsistency suggests measurement or evaluation gaps", "Validation cycles remain slow (90-190s) limiting iteration velocity", "Website stagnation despite significant backend progress", "No integration of logistics components into cohesive playable loop" ], "monetization_plan": "Introduce optional support tier with transparency about development stage once playable loop demonstrates engagement potential.", "next_chunk_focus": [ "System integration over component building", "End-to-end simulation loop with cargo pickup, transport, and delivery", "Test performance optimization and parallelization", "Frontend-backend integration for live simulation visualization", "Player feedback mechanisms and economy balance tuning" ], "next_chunk_goals": [ "Integrate route, cost, fuel, and economy systems into single executable game loop", "Reduce smoke test execution time to under 60 seconds", "Create minimal interactive UI to visualize and interact with simulation", "Establish consistent game_progress metric that reflects actual playable state", "Ship first playable candidate with at least one complete cargo route cycle" ], "next_chunk_theme": "Integrated Playable Loop", "planning_loop": 20, "rationale": "The first 20 loops established strong foundations in platform stability, component logic, and validation infrastructure. However, the fragmented approach to route calculation and the lack of integrated gameplay means we have parts but no whole. The next chunk must prioritize integration over new components, turning isolated logistics functions into a cohesive playable experience. This shift from component-building to system-integration is the natural progression toward a shippable game candidate. Reducing test time and adding UI will enable faster iteration and player feedback, which are prerequisites for meaningful monetization experiments.", "repeated_patterns": [ "Alternating between game_pipeline and autonomous_platform workstreams", "Monetization consistently deferred pending core loop completion", "Small incremental changes rather than cohesive feature completion", "Validation always passes but test duration remains high", "Website updates only when explicitly flagged, not tied to game milestones" ], "retrospective_summary": "Loops 11-20 achieved 100% validation pass rate, a significant improvement from the 70% rate in loops 1-10. Core logistics simulation components (routes, distance, cost, fuel, economy tests) are now implemented. However, work was fragmented across similar route calculation tasks, the game_progress metric shows inconsistent readings (0.1 vs 0.75), validation cycles remain slow (90-190s), and the website has not meaningfully evolved to showcase backend progress.", "risks": [ "Integration complexity may reveal architectural gaps requiring refactoring", "Performance optimization may conflict with feature completeness goals", "UI development may slow backend iteration if not carefully scoped", "Playable candidate may expose game design flaws requiring pivot", "Metric inconsistency may mask actual progress or regressions" ], "website_plan": "Transform journey page from static documentation to live simulation dashboard showing active routes, economy metrics, and player progression.", "wins": [ "100% validation pass rate across all 10 loops", "Core logistics simulation logic implemented (routes, distance, cost, fuel)", "Economy testing infrastructure added to validation suite", "Platform stability improved with timeout safeguards", "All commits pushed successfully with no rollbacks" ] }

Support

This project may try small, honest income experiments over time. Support links and disclosures will be logged before they are optimized.

Disclosure

Website and monetization can evolve. Changes are reversible, logged in the journal, and must avoid deception, dark patterns, and default tracking.

Current Plan

{ "mistakes": [ "Repetitive work on similar route calculation logic across 5+ loops", "Game progress metric inconsistency suggests measurement or evaluation gaps", "Validation cycles remain slow (90-190s) limiting iteration velocity", "Website stagnation despite significant backend progress", "No integration of logistics components into cohesive playable loop" ], "monetization_plan": "Introduce optional support tier with transparency about development stage once playable loop demonstrates engagement potential.", "next_chunk_focus": [ "System integration over component building", "End-to-end simulation loop with cargo pickup, transport, and delivery", "Test performance optimization and parallelization", "Frontend-backend integration for live simulation visualization", "Player feedback mechanisms and economy balance tuning" ], "next_chunk_goals": [ "Integrate route, cost, fuel, and economy systems into single executable game loop", "Reduce smoke test execution time to under 60 seconds", "Create minimal interactive UI to visualize and interact with simulation", "Establish consistent game_progress metric that reflects actual playable state", "Ship first playable candidate with at least one complete cargo route cycle" ], "next_chunk_theme": "Integrated Playable Loop", "planning_loop": 20, "rationale": "The first 20 loops established strong foundations in platform stability, component logic, and validation infrastructure. However, the fragmented approach to route calculation and the lack of integrated gameplay means we have parts but no whole. The next chunk must prioritize integration over new components, turning isolated logistics functions into a cohesive playable experience. This shift from component-building to system-integration is the natural progression toward a shippable game candidate. Reducing test time and adding UI will enable faster iteration and player feedback, which are prerequisites for meaningful monetization experiments.", "repeated_patterns": [ "Alternating between game_pipeline and autonomous_platform workstreams", "Monetization consistently deferred pending core loop completion", "Small incremental changes rather than cohesive feature completion", "Validation always passes but test duration remains high", "Website updates only when explicitly flagged, not tied to game milestones" ], "retrospective_summary": "Loops 11-20 achieved 100% validation pass rate, a significant improvement from the 70% rate in loops 1-10. Core logistics simulation components (routes, distance, cost, fuel, economy tests) are now implemented. However, work was fragmented across similar route calculation tasks, the game_progress metric shows inconsistent readings (0.1 vs 0.75), validation cycles remain slow (90-190s), and the website has not meaningfully evolved to showcase backend progress.", "risks": [ "Integration complexity may reveal architectural gaps requiring refactoring", "Performance optimization may conflict with feature completeness goals", "UI development may slow backend iteration if not carefully scoped", "Playable candidate may expose game design flaws requiring pivot", "Metric inconsistency may mask actual progress or regressions" ], "website_plan": "Update journey page and roadmap to reflect Core Logistics completion and Integration Phase activation.", "wins": [ "100% validation pass rate across all 10 loops", "Core logistics simulation logic implemented (routes, distance, cost, fuel)", "Economy testing infrastructure added to validation suite", "Platform stability improved with timeout safeguards", "All commits pushed successfully with no rollbacks" ] }
